name: caption-keeps-ny-source-context

input.title: No Good Burger Joint NY
input.description: Burger spot I need to try next time I'm in the city.

_comment: Extraction should preserve the explicit NY context so downstream ranking can bias to New York instead of nearby device location.

name: creator-handle-ignored-caption-text-wins

input.title: @saraheatsla on TikTok: Joe's Pizza in NYC is legendary
input.description: Plain slice perfection. #pizza #nyc

_comment: Creator handle @saraheatsla must be ignored; caption text names the restaurant directly.

name: creator-handle-only-no-strong-candidate

input.title: follow @hungryjackie for the best food recs
input.description: #foodblogger #lafood

_comment: No restaurant name in caption. Handle @hungryjackie gives no bio or address evidence. Expected: no save.

name: caption-restaurant-name-plus-unrelated-tag

input.title: dinner last night
input.description: went to Nobu Malibu with @mike_johnson, so good. #malibu

_comment: @mike_johnson is a personal tag. Restaurant name Nobu Malibu in caption must win.

name: address-in-caption-strong-signal

input.title: hidden gem in nyc
input.description: Joe's Pizza 7 Carmine St New York — still the best slice. #pizza #nyc

_comment: Caption has restaurant name + street address. AI should extract Joe's Pizza New York with high confidence.

name: multiple-handles-only-one-restaurant

input.title: taco Tuesday
input.description: @saraheatsla discovers @guerrilla_tacos — Downtown LA. #tacos

_comment: Caption text names @guerrilla_tacos with Downtown LA context. Gemini uses caption text as evidence, not handle words.

name: no-signal-show-chooser

input.title: vibes only
input.description: having fun with the crew tonight

_comment: No restaurant name, no handle, no location. Both heuristic and AI should return empty / low confidence.

name: poster-bio-evidence-beats-tagged-handle

input.title: Mad Yolks (@mad_yolks) on Instagram: “Sunday brunch done right 🍳”
input.description: Crispy fried eggs, all day brunch vibes. Eggs from @glaumeggranch 🥚 #breakfast #losangeles

_comment: Poster is @mad_yolks. If bio enrichment classifies @mad_yolks as restaurant_or_business (extractedName=Mad Yolks, extractedCity=Los Angeles), that is the primary venue. @glaumeggranch is a tagged account but the caption does not name it as the place being visited. The pipeline uses poster bio evidence first; @glaumeggranch would not be enriched as a restaurant.

name: restaurant-owned-post-uses-account-identity

input.title: weekend burger drop
input.description: 18,201 Followers, 412 Following, 899 Posts - No Good Burger Joint (@nogoodburgerjoint) on Instagram: "Open daily for smashburgers and fries"

_comment: Post text is generic. Poster account identity should become the candidate query instead of vague caption text or a random nearby place.

name: reported-reel-account-identity-needs-confirmation

input.title: stuffin grilled cheeses with smashburgers
input.description: 513,100 views, 1,100 comments - No Good Burger Joint NY (@nogoodburgerjointny) on Instagram: "Open daily for smashburgers and fries"

_comment: This mirrors the reported reel pattern: generic reel text plus poster account identity but no proven address/city in shared metadata. Query may use account identity for search, but host and edge flows must not silently save a random San Jose/Santa Cruz candidate.

name: handle-text-alone-no-bio-no-caption-name

input.title: going here this weekend 😍
input.description: check out @madyolkskitchen #brunch #eggs

_comment: Handle @madyolkskitchen looks like a restaurant name but no caption names the restaurant explicitly, no address, and no bio enrichment. Handle text alone must NOT be used as a Places query. Expected: no query, manual fallback.

name: weak-generic-food-post-no-place

input.title: best thing i ate this week
input.description: crispy, juicy, absolutely unreal. save this for later.

_comment: No place name, no address, no account identity. The pipeline should stay weak and require chooser/manual fallback instead of generating a random Places query.

name: tagged-handle-bio-evidence-poster-also-has-bio

input.title: Collab post from @mad_yolks featuring @sqirlla
input.description: Two of our favorite spots doing a brunch collab. Both have incredible eggs. #brunch #losangeles

_comment: Both @mad_yolks (poster) and @sqirlla (tagged) may have restaurant bio evidence. Caption mentions both but does not clearly name one as primary. Poster bio evidence is preferred. If both queries produce results, return ambiguous for user confirmation, not silent save.
